name: DFS Backtracking
summary: From every cell, depth-first match the word letter by letter into adjacent cells, marking the current path and undoing it when a branch fails.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the board as a grid and the word as a row of letter cells; answer starts false. loop: the scan tries each cell as a starting letter. check: at each cell the search compares the board letter to the needed word letter; a mismatch or out-of-grid move prunes the branch. update: a matched cell is marked '#' on the board (joining the current path) and restored on backtrack. recurse: the cursor dives into an adjacent cell for the next letter, the word pointer k advancing. return: answer is true if any path spells the whole word, shown by the path lit across the grid.

lineExplanations.3: Count the rows of the board.
lineExplanations.4: Count the columns of the board.
lineExplanations.5: No match found yet, so the answer starts false.
lineExplanations.6: Try starting the search from each row.
lineExplanations.7: Try starting the search from each column.
lineExplanations.8: Run a depth-first search from this cell for the whole word.
lineExplanations.9: A search succeeded — record that the word exists.
lineExplanations.11: No starting cell worked — the word is not in the grid.
lineExplanations.14: Success: every letter of the word has been matched.
lineExplanations.15: Report this path as a full match.
lineExplanations.16: If the cursor stepped off the top or bottom, this path fails.
lineExplanations.17: Report failure for an out-of-grid row.
lineExplanations.18: If the cursor stepped off the left or right, this path fails.
lineExplanations.19: Report failure for an out-of-grid column.
lineExplanations.20: If this cell's letter is not the letter we need, this path fails.
lineExplanations.21: Report failure for a letter mismatch.
lineExplanations.22: Remember this cell's letter so it can be restored later.
lineExplanations.23: Mark this cell as used ('#') so the path cannot reuse it.
lineExplanations.24: Assume this branch has not found the word yet.
lineExplanations.25: If still unfound, try stepping down.
lineExplanations.26: Search the cell below for the next letter.
lineExplanations.28: If still unfound, try stepping up.
lineExplanations.29: Search the cell above for the next letter.
lineExplanations.31: If still unfound, try stepping right.
lineExplanations.32: Search the cell to the right for the next letter.
lineExplanations.34: If still unfound, try stepping left.
lineExplanations.35: Search the cell to the left for the next letter.
lineExplanations.37: Restore this cell's letter — backtrack so other paths can use it.
lineExplanations.38: Report whether this cell started a successful path.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def exist(self, board, word): receives the grid and the target word.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(board) is the number of rows.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(board[0]) is the number of columns.
syntaxExplanations.5: answer = False starts the result as 'not found'.
syntaxExplanations.6: for sr in range(m): loops over each starting row.
syntaxExplanations.7: for sc in range(n): loops over each starting column.
syntaxExplanations.8: self.dfs(..., 0) searches from this cell, matching from letter 0.
syntaxExplanations.9: answer = True records that the word was found.
syntaxExplanations.10: return answer stops as soon as a search succeeds.
syntaxExplanations.11: return answer after every start cell has failed.
syntaxExplanations.12: A blank line separates the two methods.
syntaxExplanations.13: def dfs(self, board, word, cr, cc, k): cr,cc is the current cell, k the letter index.
syntaxExplanations.14: if k == len(word): true when all letters are matched.
syntaxExplanations.15: return True reports a complete match.
syntaxExplanations.16: if cr < 0 or cr >= len(board): 'or' tests both row edges.
syntaxExplanations.17: return False for a row outside the grid.
syntaxExplanations.18: if cc < 0 or cc >= len(board[0]): 'or' tests both column edges.
syntaxExplanations.19: return False for a column outside the grid.
syntaxExplanations.20: if board[cr][cc] != word[k]: the current letter must match the needed one.
syntaxExplanations.21: return False on a letter mismatch.
syntaxExplanations.23: board[cr][cc] = '#' marks the cell as in-use.
syntaxExplanations.24: found = False starts this cell's result as unfound.
syntaxExplanations.25: if not found: only search if we haven't found it yet.
syntaxExplanations.26: self.dfs(..., cr + 1, cc, k + 1) searches the cell below for the next letter.
syntaxExplanations.27: found = True records success from below.
syntaxExplanations.28: if not found: try the next direction only if still unfound.
syntaxExplanations.29: self.dfs(..., cr - 1, cc, k + 1) searches the cell above.
syntaxExplanations.30: found = True records success from above.
syntaxExplanations.31: if not found: try the next direction only if still unfound.
syntaxExplanations.32: self.dfs(..., cr, cc + 1, k + 1) searches the cell to the right.
syntaxExplanations.33: found = True records success from the right.
syntaxExplanations.34: if not found: try the last direction only if still unfound.
syntaxExplanations.35: self.dfs(..., cr, cc - 1, k + 1) searches the cell to the left.
syntaxExplanations.36: found = True records success from the left.
syntaxExplanations.38: return found reports this cell's result to its caller.
